stem: Which choice most effectively combines the underlined information to support the student's argument?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Urban green spaces benefit community health through multiple pathways: they filter air pollution, provide venues for exercise and social connection, and create natural cooling systems that reduce urban heat by up to 5°F.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Green spaces reduce air pollution and provide exercise opportunities, while residents near parks report 23% lower stress levels and cities with more green space have lower respiratory illness rates.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Parks and gardens filter harmful particles from the air, and they also reduce urban heat island effects by up to 5°F, plus residents living near them have 23% lower stress levels.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Urban green spaces reduce air pollution, lower stress levels by 23%, provide exercise opportunities, reduce respiratory illness rates, and create cooling systems that reduce heat by 5°F.

explanation: Choice A most effectively combines the information by organizing the benefits into clear categories (air quality, physical/social opportunities, and temperature regulation) using a logical structure with a colon to introduce the list. This approach emphasizes the 'multiple pathways' concept, which strengthens the argument for prioritizing green space development by showing the comprehensive nature of the benefits.

distractorRationale.B: While factually accurate, this choice creates an awkward structure by mixing general benefits with specific statistics, making the argument less compelling and harder to follow.
distractorRationale.C: This choice uses informal connecting words ('plus') and presents information in a choppy, list-like manner that doesn't effectively build the argument's persuasive power.
distractorRationale.D: Although comprehensive, this choice becomes a simple catalog of benefits without organizational structure, missing the opportunity to show how these benefits work together as a cohesive argument.

passage:
While researching the impact of urban green spaces on community health, a student has gathered the following notes:

• Parks and community gardens reduce air pollution by filtering harmful particles
• Residents living near green spaces report 23% lower stress levels than those in areas without parks
• Urban green areas provide opportunities for physical exercise and social interaction
• Cities with more green space per capita have significantly lower rates of respiratory illness
• Green spaces serve as natural cooling systems, reducing urban heat island effects by up to 5°F

The student wants to write an essay arguing that cities should prioritize the development of urban green spaces.
